title: Ceropegia macroloba
intro: Ceropegia macroloba, also known as the ropevine or rope plant, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ceropegia, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia macroloba include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ceropegia macroloba include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.

benefits: Ceropegia macroloba, also known as the 'Hanging Rope Vine', has been studied for its potential health benefits.
